titlestmt.titleproper: Lutheran and Moravian churches of Brooklyn collection 1986.031
titlestmt.author: Weatherly Stephan
titlestmt.sponsor: This collection was originally processed and described as part of the project, "Uncovering the Secrets of Brooklyn's 19th Century Past: Creation to Consolidation," funded by the Council on Library and Information Resources, with additional support from The Gladys Krieble Delmas Foundation.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-06 20:39:15 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.unittitle: Lutheran and Moravian churches of Brooklyn collection
did.abstract: Newsletters and periodicals, church service programs, ledgers, and printed ephemera from Lutheran and Moravian Brooklyn churches.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Source and date for the majority of the collection is unknown. This collection was formally accessioned in 1986. The Good Shepherd Lutheran Church ledger is CBH accession 2025.042.

bioghist.head: Historical note
bioghist.p: Lutherans arrived in the New Amsterdam colony among Dutch settlers in 1649. Though the practice of Lutheranism was not explicitly forbidden, hostility from the Dutch Reformed church slowed the establishment and growth of the denomination in the colony. As German immigrants arrived in the 18th century, tensions arose between German and Dutch Lutherans, primarily over the language in which services would be delivered. By 1840, Brooklyn had seven Lutheran chuches. The denomination grew again with an influx of German and Scandinavian immigrants, making Lutherans the second largest Protestant congregation in New York City in 1920.

Moravians are a Protestant denomination which originated from the Moravian region in what is now the Czech Republic. The first Moravian church in the area of present-day New York City was dedicated in 1763. By the 1990s, New York City had 10 Moravian churches, two of which were located in Brooklyn.

list.head: Sources
list.item: Erickson, Alana J. "Lutherans." In The Encyclopedia of New York City, ed. Kenneth T. Jackson, 700-701. New Haven: Yale University Press; New York: New-York Historical Society, 1995.
"Moravian church." In The Encyclopedia of New York City, ed. Kenneth T. Jackson, 769. New Haven: Yale University Press; New York: New-York Historical Society, 1995.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); Lutheran and Moravian churches of Brooklyn collection, 1986.031, Box and Folder number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Originally minimally processed to the collection level, this collection was reprocessed by Dee Bowers in 2026 as part of integrating a newly acquired ledger from the Church of the Good Shepherd (CBH accession 2025.042).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Lutheran and Moravian churches of Brooklyn includes newsletters and periodicals, church service programs, ledgers, and printed ephemera from Advent Lutheran Church, Good Shepherd Lutheran Church, Lutheran Church of the Redeemer, Lutheran Church of the Reformation, St. Stephen's Evangelical Lutheran Church, and Moravian Church of Brooklyn.

controlaccess.geogname: Brooklyn (New York, N.Y.) -- Religious life and customs

Lutherans -- New York (State) -- Kings County

Lutheran Church of the Redeemer (Brooklyn, New York, N.Y.)
Good Shepherd Lutheran Church (Brooklyn, New York, N.Y.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One ledger with Church Council meeting minutes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 40th anniversary book and tinted photo of the church with inset of pastor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concert program (1876),Sunday School Christmas hymn sheets (1881), Sunday School benefit event program (1882).
